title: Dense Bi-Encoder Retrieval and Domain Transfer · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release board compares near-miss decisions for dense bi-encoder retrieval and domain transfer and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, safe abstention, explicit uncertainty, and named native-model, vector-store, provider, load, security, recovery, or production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for dense retrieval candidate service. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Separate offline document encoding from online query encoding while preserving one versioned bi-encoder space.
Apply model-specific query and document prompts, prefixes, normalization, and maximum-length behavior.
Tune candidate depth from recall and downstream reranking evidence rather than context-window size.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Rag Embedding Migration
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Rag Embedding Migration: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz RAG engineering team handles case 2176 during a public-benefits policy change. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate dense retrieval on one embedding space, candidate recall, domain, and language evidence; measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score; then replace one exact identifier with a synonym and compare lexical, dense, and hybrid recall. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Plan dual encoding, reindexing, compatibility, canaries, cache invalidation, rollback, and old-vector deletion across model changes.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Rag Embedding Migration.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RAG and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Rag Embedding Migration, keep Rag Embedding Migration connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.
